title: Users can see and manage Gravatar-backed avatars
description:
Feature: Gravatar-backed dashboard avatars

As a dashboard user, I want my sidebar avatar to come from Gravatar and my settings page to tell me where to update it, so that my profile image is consistent and self-service.

Scenario: Sidebar shows the user's Gravatar
Given I am signed in with an email address
When I view the dashboard sidebar user identity area
Then the avatar image is loaded from Gravatar for that email address
And the user name remains visible beside the avatar

Scenario: Settings explain where to change the avatar
Given I am signed in with an email address
When I open user settings
Then I see the same Gravatar-backed avatar context
And I can follow a link to Gravatar to update the profile image

Out of scope:
- Uploading avatar images directly in Plexus
- Storing avatar binaries in Plexus
- Adding alternate avatar providers or fallback provider chains

comments.author: ryan
comments.text: Implementation note: added the required schema slice for user attribution: new User model using Cognito sub as id, nullable createdByUserId belongsTo relationships on Evaluation, Procedure, Report, ScoreVersion, and ChatMessage, plus the minimum relationship indexes. Prioritizing a staging deployment rehearsal of this schema before waiting on full CI, per user direction.

comments.author: ryan
comments.text: Validation note: backend schema TypeScript compile passed on feature branch. Cherry-picked the schema commit onto the current staging cleanup branch in an isolated worktree and resolved the staging-only resource.ts conflict without reintroducing removed cleanup resources. Staging worktree backend compile also passed after linking existing dashboard node_modules.

comments.author: ryan
comments.text: Staging rehearsal result: Amplify job 131 failed during CDK assembly before deploy because ConnectionStack synthesized 505 resources, 5 over the 500-resource CloudFormation stack limit. Breakdown: 336 AppSync FunctionConfiguration, 168 AppSync Resolver, 1 CDK Metadata. Next test removes the ChatMessage User relationship and createdByUserId index while keeping ChatMessage.createdByUserId as a scalar attribution field; local backend schema compile passes with that reduction.

comments.author: ryan
comments.text: Staging schema rehearsal succeeded. Amplify job 132 completed successfully on staging after changing ChatMessage attribution to scalar-only createdByUserId. ConnectionStack is now 499 resources (332 AppSync functions, 166 resolvers, 1 metadata). Proceeding with schema-only promotion path; Gravatar UI changes remain out of this release.

comments.author: ryan
comments.text: Created PR #286 from develop to main for the schema release: https://github.com/AnthusAI/Plexus/pull/286. The release includes the staging-proven User attribution schema with ChatMessage scalar-only attribution.

comments.author: ryan
comments.text: CI follow-up for PR #286: pushed fix commit to develop for dashboard generated relation typing and the MCP documentation lookup failure exposed by the develop-to-main PR checks. Local dashboard ci:typecheck now passes; MCP docs tool was smoke-tested manually with optimizer-cookbook.

comments.author: ryan
comments.text: PR #286 CI passed after the follow-up fix. Checks passing include Dashboard Unit Tests, Dashboard Storybook Tests, Python Tests 3.11/3.12, CodeQL analyses, version sync, and Snyk.

comments.author: ryan
comments.text: Accepted PR #286 code-quality suggestion by removing unused import in scripts/migrate_agent_docs.py (commit 4e4d0e2a on develop).
